html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eighteen — Part Two

The Book of Yirmeyahu

YIRMEYAHU

The Book of Jeremiah — DSS Shorter Recension Primary

Chapters 8 through 15

The Harvest Is Past — Do Not Learn the Way of the Nations — The Linen Loincloth — Your Words Were My Joy

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJer-a and 4QJer-b — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTERS 8 THROUGH 15 — THE COVENANT LAMENTS AND THE PROPHETIC CALL

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 8 The Harvest Is Past and We Are Not Saved — The Wise Men Are Put to Shame — Peace Peace When There Is No Peace

1 At that time, declares YHWH, the bones of the kings of Yehudah, the bones of its officials, the bones of the priests, the bones of the prophets, and the bones of the inhabitants of Yerushalayim shall be brought out of their tombs.

2 And they shall be spread before the sun and the moon and all the host of heaven, which they have loved and served, which they have gone after, and which they have sought and worshiped. They shall not be gathered or buried. They shall be as dung on the surface of the ground.

3 Death shall be preferred to life by all the remnant that remains of this evil family in all the places where I have driven them, declares YHWH of hosts.

4 You shall say to them, Thus says YHWH: When men fall, do they not rise again? If one turns away, does he not return?

5 Why then has this people turned away in perpetual backsliding? They hold fast to deceit — they refuse to return.

6 I have paid attention and listened, but they have not spoken rightly. No one repents of his evil, saying, What have I done? Everyone turns to his own course, like a horse plunging headlong into battle.

7 Even the stork in the heavens knows her times, and the turtledove, swallow, and crane keep the time of their migration, but my people know not the rules of YHWH.

8 How can you say, We are wise, and the Torah of YHWH is with us? But behold, the lying pen of the scribes has made it into a lie.

9 The wise men shall be put to shame — they shall be dismayed and taken. Behold, they have rejected the word of YHWH, so what wisdom is in them?

10 Therefore I will give their wives to others and their fields to conquerors, because from the least to the greatest everyone is greedy for unjust gain. From prophet to priest, everyone deals falsely.

11 They have healed the wound of my people lightly, saying, Peace, peace, when there is no peace.

12 Were they ashamed when they committed abomination? No, they were not at all ashamed — they did not know how to blush. Therefore they shall fall among the fallen. When I punish them, they shall be overthrown, says YHWH.

13 When I would gather them, declares YHWH, there are no grapes on the vine, nor figs on the fig tree — even the leaves are withered, and what I gave them has passed away from them.

14 Why do we sit still? Gather together — let us go into the fortified cities and perish there, for YHWH our Creator has doomed us to perish and has given us poisoned water to drink, because we have sinned against YHWH.

15 We looked for peace, but no good came — for a time of healing, but behold, terror.

16 The snorting of their horses is heard from Dan — at the sound of the neighing of their stallions the whole land quakes. They come and devour the land and all that fills it, the city and those who dwell in it.

17 For behold, I am sending among you serpents, adders that cannot be charmed, and they shall bite you, declares YHWH.

18 My joy is gone — grief is upon me — my heart is sick within me.

19 Behold, the cry of the daughter of my people from the length and breadth of the land: Is YHWH not in Tziyon? Is her King not in her? Why have they provoked me to anger with their carved images and with their foreign idols?

20 The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.

21 For the wound of the daughter of my people is my heart wounded — I mourn, and dismay has taken hold on me.

22 Is there no balm in Gil'ad? Is there no physician there? Why then has the health of the daughter of my people not been restored?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves portions of Chapter 8. The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic for this chapter. Verse 11 — peace, peace, when there is no peace — repeats the covenant indictment of Chapter 6:14 word for word, establishing it as Yirmeyahu's signature covenant accusation against false prophecy.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 22 — is there no balm in Gil'ad? Is there no physician there? — is one of the most quoted covenant lament questions in the entire prophetic corpus. The rhetorical question exposes the covenant tragedy: the healing exists, the physician is present, but the patient refuses the covenant cure.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 8. Verse 7 — even the stork in the heavens knows her times, and the turtledove, swallow, and crane keep the time of their migration, but my people know not the rules of YHWH — is the covenant indictment from creation: migratory birds have more covenant knowledge than YHWH's covenant people.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20 — the harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved. The covenant lament of lost opportunity: the agricultural seasons — harvest and summer — are the covenant windows for covenant response. Both have closed. The covenant community waited through both seasons expecting salvation without covenant return. Verse 21 — for the wound of the daughter of my people is my heart wounded. The prophet's grief for the covenant community is not professional distance but covenant identification: their wound is his wound.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 9 Oh That My Head Were Waters — They Have Taught Their Tongue to Speak Lies — Let Him Who Glories Glory in This

1 Oh that my head were waters, and my eyes a fountain of tears, that I might weep day and night for the slain of the daughter of my people.

2 Oh that I had in the desert a travelers' lodging place, that I might leave my people and go away from them. For they are all adulterers, a company of treacherous men.

3 They bend their tongue like a bow — falsehood and not truth has grown strong in the land. For they proceed from evil to evil, and they do not know me, declares YHWH.

4 Let everyone beware of his neighbor, and put no trust in any brother, for every brother is a deceiver, and every neighbor goes about as a slanderer.

5 Everyone deceives his neighbor, and no one speaks the truth. They have taught their tongue to speak lies — they weary themselves committing iniquity.

6 Heaping oppression upon oppression, and deceit upon deceit, they refuse to know me, declares YHWH.

7 Therefore thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, I will refine them and test them, for what else can I do, because of my people?

8 Their tongue is a deadly arrow — it speaks deceit. With his mouth each speaks peace to his neighbor, but in his heart he plans an ambush for him.

9 Shall I not punish them for these things? declares YHWH. And shall I not avenge myself on a nation such as this?

10 I will take up weeping and wailing for the mountains, and a lamentation for the pastures of the wilderness, because they are laid waste so that no one passes through, and the lowing of cattle is not heard. Both the birds of the air and the beasts have fled and are gone.

11 I will make Yerushalayim a heap of ruins, a lair of jackals. And I will make the cities of Yehudah a desolation, without inhabitant.

12 Who is the man so wise that he can understand this? To whom has the mouth of YHWH spoken, that he may declare it? Why is the land ruined and laid waste like a wilderness, so that no one passes through?

13 And YHWH says: Because they have forsaken my Torah that I set before them, and have not obeyed my voice or walked in accord with it,

14 But have stubbornly followed their own hearts and have gone after the Ba'alim, as their fathers taught them.

15 Therefore thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I will feed this people with bitter food, and give them poisonous water to drink.

16 I will scatter them among the nations whom neither they nor their fathers have known, and I will send the sword after them, until I have consumed them.

17 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Consider, and call for the mourning women to come — send for the skilled women to come.

18 Let them make haste and raise a wailing over us, that our eyes may run down with tears and our eyelids flow with water.

19 For a sound of wailing is heard from Tziyon: How we are ruined — we are utterly shamed, because we have left the land, because they have cast down our dwellings.

20 Hear, O women, the word of YHWH, and let your ear receive the word of his mouth. Teach to your daughters a lament, and each to her neighbor a dirge.

21 For death has come up into our windows — it has entered our palaces, cutting off the children from the streets and the young men from the squares.

22 Speak: Thus declares YHWH — The dead bodies of men shall fall like dung upon the open field, like cut grain after the reaper, and none shall gather them.

23 Thus says YHWH: Let not the wise man boast in his wisdom, let not the mighty man boast in his might, let not the rich man boast in his riches,

24 But let him who boasts boast in this, that he understands and knows me, that I am YHWH who practices steadfast love, justice, and righteousness in the earth. For in these things I delight, declares YHWH.

25 Behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will punish all those who are circumcised merely in the flesh —

26 Egypt, Yehudah, Edom, the sons of Ammon, Moav, and all who dwell in the desert who cut the corners of their hair, for all these nations are uncircumcised, and all the house of Yisra'EL are uncircumcised in heart.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-b preserves portions of Chapter 9. The DSS shorter recension presents Chapter 9 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 24 — let him who boasts boast in this, that he understands and knows me, that I am YHWH who practices steadfast love, justice, and righteousness — is the covenant definition of the only legitimate boast: covenant knowledge of YHWH's character.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 1 — oh that my head were waters, and my eyes a fountain of tears — is read as the covenant expression of prophetic grief that exceeds human capacity: the suffering Yirmeyahu witnesses and announces surpasses what human tears can adequately mourn.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 9. Verse 23-24 — let not the wise man boast in his wisdom, let not the mighty man boast in his might — but let him who boasts boast in this, that he understands and knows me — is the covenant counterculture declaration: YHWH's covenant economy inverts the world's measures of value and status.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3-6 — they have taught their tongue to speak lies — they weary themselves committing iniquity — they refuse to know me. The covenant diagnosis of the lying tongue: lying is not merely a habit but a curriculum. The covenant community has actively taught its tongue falsehood. The covenant consequence — they do not know YHWH — is the direct result: a community whose internal communication is structured by lies cannot know a Creator whose covenant is defined by truth.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 10 Do Not Learn the Way of the Nations — Idols Are Like Scarecrows — YHWH Is the True Creator — The Tent Is Destroyed

1 Hear the word that YHWH speaks to you, O house of Yisra'EL.

2 Thus says YHWH: Learn not the way of the nations, nor be dismayed at the signs of the heavens because the nations are dismayed at them,

3 For the customs of the peoples are vanity. A tree from the forest is cut down and worked with an axe by the hands of a craftsman.

4 They decorate it with silver and gold. They fasten it with hammer and nails so that it cannot move.

5 Their idols are like scarecrows in a cucumber field, and they cannot speak. They have to be carried, for they cannot walk. Do not be afraid of them, for they cannot do evil, neither is it in them to do good.

6 There is none like you, O YHWH — you are great, and your name is great in might.

7 Who would not fear you, O King of the nations? For this is your due — for among all the wise ones of the nations and in all their kingdoms there is none like you.

8 They are both stupid and foolish — the instruction of idols is but wood.

9 Beaten silver is brought from Tarshish, and gold from Ufaz. They are the work of the craftsman and of the hands of the goldsmith. Their clothing is violet and purple — they are all the work of skilled men.

10 But YHWH is the true Creator — he is the living Creator and the everlasting King. At his wrath the earth quakes, and the nations cannot endure his indignation.

11 Thus shall you say to them: The gods who did not make the heavens and the earth shall perish from the earth and from under the heavens.

12 It is he who made the earth by his power, who established the world by his wisdom, and by his understanding stretched out the heavens.

13 When he utters his voice, there is a tumult of waters in the heavens, and he makes the mist rise from the ends of the earth. He makes lightning for the rain, and he brings forth the wind from his storehouses.

14 Every man is stupid and without knowledge — every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols, for his images are false, and there is no breath in them.

15 They are worthless, a work of delusion. At the time of their punishment they shall perish.

16 Not like these is he who is the portion of Yaakov, for he is the one who formed all things, and Yisra'EL is the tribe of his inheritance. YHWH of hosts is his name.

17 Gather up your bundle from the ground, O you who dwell under siege.

18 For thus says YHWH: Behold, I am slinging out the inhabitants of the land at this time, and I will bring distress on them, that they may feel it.

19 Woe is me because of my hurt — my wound is grievous. But I said, Truly this is an affliction, and I must bear it.

20 My tent is destroyed, and all my cords are broken. My children have gone from me, and they are not — there is no one to spread my tent again and to set up my curtains.

21 For the shepherds are stupid and do not inquire of YHWH. Therefore they have not prospered, and all their flock is scattered.

22 A voice — a rumor — behold, it comes. A great commotion out of the land of the north, to make the cities of Yehudah a desolation, a lair of jackals.

23 I know, O YHWH, that the way of man is not in himself, that it is not in man who walks to direct his steps.

24 Correct me, O YHWH, but in justice — not in your anger, lest you bring me to nothing.

25 Pour out your wrath on the nations that know you not, and on the peoples that call not on your name, for they have devoured Yaakov — they have devoured him and consumed him, and have laid waste his habitation.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves portions of Chapter 10. Notably, the DSS shorter recension of Chapter 10 lacks verses 6-8 and verse 10 that appear in the Masoretic — the DSS text moves directly from verse 5 to verse 9 and then to verse 11. This is one of the clearest demonstrations of the DSS shorter recension differing from the Masoretic. The idol satire of verses 1-5 is preserved in both.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 10 — but YHWH is the true Creator — he is the living Creator and the everlasting King — is read as the covenant confession that corresponds to the idol catalog of verses 3-9: the Creator who made all things by his power contrasts absolutely with the made things that their makers carry.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves a longer form of Chapter 10 than the DSS. Verses 12-16 — the creation hymn — appear also in Yirmeyahu 51:15-19. The covenant doxology embedded within the idol polemic declares YHWH's creative power as the ground of idol worship's absurdity: the creator of everything cannot be compared with the things his creatures have made.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23-24 — I know, O YHWH, that the way of man is not in himself — that it is not in man who walks to direct his steps. Correct me in justice, not in anger. The covenant confession within the lament: Yirmeyahu acknowledges the human inability to self-direct — the covenant community's failure is not merely moral but ontological. Covenant guidance from YHWH is not optional — it is the only path that does not end in destruction. The prayer for correction rather than anger is the covenant theology of discipline: receive the covenant correction before the covenant judgment arrives.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 11 The Covenant with Yisra'EL — Cursed Is the Man Who Does Not Obey — A Conspiracy — Do Not Pray for This People

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH:

2 Hear the words of this covenant, and speak to the men of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.

3 You shall say to them, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Cursed be the man who does not hear the words of this covenant

4 That I commanded your fathers when I brought them out of the land of Egypt, from the iron furnace, saying, Listen to my voice, and do all that I command you. So shall you be my people, and I will be your Creator,

5 That I may confirm the oath that I swore to your fathers, to give them a land flowing with milk and honey, as at this day. Then I answered, So be it, YHWH.

6 And YHWH said to me, Proclaim all these words in the cities of Yehudah and in the streets of Yerushalayim: Hear the words of this covenant and do them.

7 For I solemnly warned your fathers when I brought them up out of the land of Egypt, warning them persistently, even to this day, saying, Obey my voice.

8 Yet they did not obey or incline their ear, but everyone walked in the stubbornness of his evil heart. Therefore I brought upon them all the words of this covenant, which I commanded them to do, but they did not.

9 Again YHWH said to me, A conspiracy exists among the men of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.

10 They have turned back to the iniquities of their forefathers, who refused to hear my words. They have gone after other gods to serve them. The house of Yisra'EL and the house of Yehudah have broken my covenant that I made with their fathers.

11 Therefore, thus says YHWH: Behold, I am bringing disaster upon them that they cannot escape. Though they cry to me, I will not listen to them.

12 Then the cities of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim will go and cry to the gods to whom they make offerings, but they cannot save them in the time of their trouble.

13 For your gods have become as many as your cities, O Yehudah, and as many as the streets of Yerushalayim are the altars you have set up to shame, altars to make offerings to Ba'al.

14 Therefore do not pray for this people, or lift up a cry or prayer on their behalf, for I will not listen when they call to me in the time of their trouble.

15 What right has my beloved in my house, when she has done many vile deeds? Can even sacrificial flesh avert your doom? Can you then exult?

16 YHWH once called you a green olive tree, beautiful with good fruit. But with the roar of a great tempest he will set fire to it, and its branches will be consumed.

17 YHWH of hosts, who planted you, has decreed disaster against you, because of the evil that the house of Yisra'EL and the house of Yehudah have done, provoking me to anger by making offerings to Ba'al.

18 YHWH made it known to me and I knew. Then you showed me their deeds.

19 But I was like a gentle lamb led to the slaughter. I did not know it was against me they devised schemes, saying, Let us destroy the tree with its fruit — let us cut him off from the land of the living, that his name be remembered no more.

20 But, O YHWH of hosts, who judges righteously, who tests the heart and the mind, let me see your vengeance upon them, for to you have I committed my cause.

21 Therefore thus says YHWH concerning the men of Anatot, who seek your life, and say, Do not prophesy in the name of YHWH, or you will die by our hand —

22 Therefore thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, I will punish them. The young men shall die by the sword. Their sons and their daughters shall die by famine,

23 And none of them shall be left. For I will bring disaster upon the men of Anatot, the year of their punishment.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves portions of Chapter 11. The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic for this chapter. The covenant lawsuit of Chapter 11 formalizes what Chapters 2-10 have described: the covenant made at Sinai — verse 4 — has been systematically violated, and YHWH is now executing the covenant curses of Devarim. Verse 19 — I was like a gentle lamb led to the slaughter — is Yirmeyahu's personal covenant suffering that parallels the servant theology of Yesha'yahu 53.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verses 21-23 — the men of Anatot seek Yirmeyahu's life and command him not to prophesy — is read as the first of the covenant persecutions Yirmeyahu suffers from his own priestly community. Anatot was Yirmeyahu's hometown — the rejection begins at home.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 11. Verse 14 — do not pray for this people, for I will not listen when they call to me in the time of their trouble — is the covenant command to the interceding prophet that his intercession is suspended. This is one of the most theologically severe covenant declarations in the book: YHWH withdrawing from Yirmeyahu his prophetic function of intercession.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19 — I did not know it was against me they devised schemes — let us destroy the tree with its fruit. The covenant community of Anatot — Yirmeyahu's own priestly clan — plots his death. The covenant pattern of the rejected prophet begins with those closest to him. The lamb that does not resist the slaughter is the covenant image that defines Yirmeyahu's prophetic posture throughout the book: he will not silence his covenant word even when the consequence is death.

THE COVENANT THEODICY AND SIGN ACTS — CHAPTERS 12 THROUGH 15

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 12 Why Does the Way of the Wicked Prosper — My Heritage Has Become Like a Lion — I Will Return and Have Compassion

1 Righteous are you, O YHWH, when I complain to you. Yet I would plead my case before you: Why does the way of the wicked prosper? Why do all who are treacherous thrive?

2 You plant them, and they take root. They grow and produce fruit. You are near in their mouth and far from their heart.

3 But you, O YHWH, know me — you see me, and test my heart toward you. Pull them out like sheep for the slaughter, and set them apart for the day of slaughter.

4 How long will the land mourn and the grass of every field wither? For the evil of those who dwell in it the beasts and the birds are swept away, because they said, He will not see our latter end.

5 If you have raced with men on foot, and they have wearied you, how will you compete with horses? And if in a safe land you are so trusting, what will you do in the thicket of the Yarden?

6 For even your brothers and the house of your father, even they have dealt treacherously with you — they are in full cry after you. Do not believe them, though they speak friendly words to you.

7 I have forsaken my house — I have abandoned my heritage. I have given the beloved of my soul into the hands of her enemies.

8 My heritage has become to me like a lion in the forest. She has lifted up her voice against me — therefore I hate her.

9 Is my heritage to me like a speckled bird of prey? Are the birds of prey against her all around? Go, assemble all the wild beasts — bring them to devour.

10 Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard — they have trampled down my portion. They have made my pleasant portion a desolate wilderness.

11 They have made it a desolation — desolate, it mourns to me. The whole land is made desolate, but no man lays it to heart.

12 Upon all the bare heights in the desert destroyers have come, for the sword of YHWH devours from one end of the land to the other. No flesh has peace.

13 They have sown wheat and have reaped thorns. They have tired themselves out but profit nothing. They shall be ashamed of their harvests because of the fierce anger of YHWH.

14 Thus says YHWH concerning all my evil neighbors who touch the heritage that I have given my people Yisra'EL to inherit: Behold, I will pluck them up from their land, and I will pluck up the house of Yehudah from among them.

15 And after I have plucked them up, I will again have compassion on them, and I will bring them again each to his heritage and each to his land.

16 And it shall come to pass, if they will diligently learn the ways of my people, to swear by my name, As YHWH lives, even as they taught my people to swear by Ba'al, then they shall be built up in the midst of my people.

17 But if any nation will not listen, then I will utterly pluck it up and destroy it, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves portions of Chapter 12. The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 12 contains Yirmeyahu's first major covenant complaint — the theodicy question that will recur throughout the book: why do the wicked prosper? YHWH's answer — verse 5 — is not an explanation but a covenant challenge: if you cannot handle this, how will you handle what is coming?

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verses 14-16 — even the evil neighbors who touched Yisra'EL's inheritance will receive compassion and restoration if they learn YHWH's ways — is the covenant universalism embedded within the covenant judgment: YHWH's restoration purposes extend even to the nations that were instruments of the covenant punishment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 12. Verse 5 — if you have raced with men on foot, and they have wearied you, how will you compete with horses? — is YHWH's covenant response to Yirmeyahu's theodicy complaint. The answer to the question of why the wicked prosper is not philosophical but practical: prepare for greater trials, not easier ones.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — righteous are you, O YHWH, when I complain to you. Yet I would plead my case before you. The covenant boldness of Yirmeyahu's prayer: he simultaneously affirms YHWH's righteousness and demands a covenant explanation. This is covenant prayer at its most honest — the prophet does not suppress the covenant question but takes it directly to the covenant court. Verse 7 — I have forsaken my house — I have abandoned my heritage. YHWH speaks his own grief: the abandonment of the covenant community is not cold judicial action but the painful decision of a covenant partner who has been betrayed.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 13 The Linen Loincloth — Spoiled and Profitable for Nothing — Can the Cushite Change His Skin — Woe to You O Yerushalayim

1 Thus says YHWH to me: Go and buy a linen loincloth and put it around your waist, and do not dip it in water.

2 So I bought a loincloth according to the word of YHWH, and put it around my waist.

3 And the word of YHWH came to me a second time,

4 Take the loincloth that you have bought, which is around your waist, and arise, go to the Euphrates and hide it there in a cleft of the rock.

5 So I went and hid it by the Euphrates, as YHWH commanded me.

6 And after many days YHWH said to me, Arise, go to the Euphrates, and take from there the loincloth that I commanded you to hide there.

7 Then I went to the Euphrates, and dug, and I took the loincloth from the place where I had hidden it. And behold, the loincloth was spoiled — it was good for nothing.

8 Then the word of YHWH came to me:

9 Thus says YHWH: Even so will I spoil the pride of Yehudah and the great pride of Yerushalayim.

10 This evil people, who refuse to hear my words, who stubbornly follow their own heart and have gone after other gods to serve them and worship them, shall be like this loincloth, which is good for nothing.

11 For as the loincloth clings to the waist of a man, so I made the whole house of Yisra'EL and the whole house of Yehudah cling to me, declares YHWH, that they might be for me a people, a name, a praise, and a glory, but they would not listen.

12 You shall speak to them this word: Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, Every jar shall be filled with wine. And they will say to you, Do we not indeed know that every jar will be filled with wine?

13 Then you shall say to them, Thus says YHWH: Behold, I will fill with drunkenness all the inhabitants of this land: the kings who sit on Dawid's throne, the priests, the prophets, and all the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.

14 And I will dash them one against another, fathers and sons together, declares YHWH. I will not pity or spare or have compassion, that I should not destroy them.

15 Hear and give ear — be not proud, for YHWH has spoken.

16 Give glory to YHWH your Creator before he brings darkness, before your feet stumble on the twilight mountains, and while you look for light he turns it into gloom and makes it deep darkness.

17 But if you will not listen, my soul will weep in secret for your pride — my eyes will weep bitterly and run down with tears, because the flock of YHWH has been taken captive.

18 Say to the king and the queen mother: Take a lowly seat, for your beautiful crown has come down from your head.

19 The cities of the Negev are shut up, with none to open them. All of Yehudah is taken into exile — wholly taken into exile.

20 Lift up your eyes and see those who come from the north. Where is the flock that was given you, your beautiful flock?

21 What will you say when they set as head over you those whom you yourself have taught to be friends to you? Will not pangs take hold of you like those of a woman in labor?

22 And if you say in your heart, Why have these things come upon me? It is for the greatness of your iniquity that your skirts are lifted up and you suffer violence.

23 Can the Kushite change his skin or the leopard his spots? Then also you can do good who are accustomed to do evil.

24 I will scatter you like chaff driven by the wind from the desert.

25 This is your lot, the portion I have measured out to you, declares YHWH, because you have forgotten me and trusted in lies.

26 I myself will lift up your skirts over your face, and your shame will be seen.

27 I have seen your abominations, your adulteries and neighings, your lewd whorings, on the hills in the field. Woe to you, O Yerushalayim — how long will it be before you are made clean?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves portions of Chapter 13. The linen loincloth sign-act — verses 1-11 — is one of the most elaborate covenant sign-acts in the book. Whether the Euphrates journey was physical or visionary has been debated, but the covenant meaning is clear: Yisra'EL was made to cling to YHWH as a loincloth clings to a man's body — intimate, functional covenant relationship. Separated from YHWH and buried in alien ground, the covenant people become useless.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 23 — can the Kushite change his skin or the leopard his spots? Then also you can do good who are accustomed to do evil — is read as the covenant declaration of the depth of covenant corruption: the evil has become so habitual that self-reformation is as impossible as changing one's melanin or a leopard's spots. Only covenant transformation from outside can accomplish what self-effort cannot.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 13. Verse 11 — I made the whole house of Yisra'EL and Yehudah cling to me that they might be for me a people, a name, a praise, and a glory — the covenant definition of Yisra'EL's purpose: covenant glory for YHWH through covenant intimacy with him. The loincloth that is useless away from the body had a function it failed to fulfill.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — if you will not listen, my soul will weep in secret for your pride. The covenant weeping of YHWH through the prophet: the grief is private, secret — not performed for the audience but real in the covenant relationship. Verse 23 — can the Kushite change his skin? The covenant statement on the depth of human moral corruption: the capacity for covenant good has been so thoroughly suppressed by covenant habit of evil that the community cannot reverse itself. This is the covenant ground for the new covenant promise of Yirmeyahu 31:31-34 — a covenant that writes the Torah on the heart rather than on stone.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 14 The Great Drought — The Prophets Prophesy Lies — YHWH Has Rejected and Forsaken This Generation — O YHWH Act for Your Name's Sake

1 The word of YHWH that came to Yirmeyahu concerning the drought:

2 Yehudah mourns, and her gates languish. Her people lament on the ground, and the cry of Yerushalayim goes up.

3 Her nobles send their servants for water. They come to the cisterns — they find no water. They return with their vessels empty. They are ashamed and confounded and cover their heads.

4 Because the ground is cracked — for there has been no rain on the land — the farmers are ashamed. They cover their heads.

5 Even the doe in the field forsakes her newborn fawn because there is no grass.

6 The wild donkeys stand on the bare heights — they pant for air like jackals. Their eyes fail because there is no vegetation.

7 Though our iniquities testify against us, act, O YHWH, for your name's sake. For our backslidings are many — we have sinned against you.

8 O you hope of Yisra'EL, its savior in time of trouble, why should you be like a stranger in the land, like a traveler who turns aside to tarry for a night?

9 Why should you be like a man confused, like a mighty warrior who cannot save? Yet you, O YHWH, are in the midst of us, and we are called by your name — do not leave us.

10 Thus says YHWH concerning this people: They have loved to wander. They have not restrained their feet. Therefore YHWH does not accept them. Now he will remember their iniquity and punish their sins.

11 YHWH said to me, Do not pray for the welfare of this people.

12 Though they fast, I will not hear their cry, and though they offer burnt offering and grain offering, I will not accept them. But I will consume them by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence.

13 Then I said, Ah, YHWH my Creator, behold, the prophets say to them, You shall not see the sword, nor shall you have famine, but I will give you assured peace in this place.

14 And YHWH said to me, The prophets are prophesying lies in my name. I did not send them, nor did I command them or speak to them. They are prophesying to you a lying vision, worthless divination, and the deceit of their own minds.

15 Therefore thus says YHWH concerning the prophets who prophesy in my name although I did not send them, and who say, Sword and famine shall not come upon this land: By sword and famine those prophets shall be consumed.

16 And the people to whom they prophesy shall be cast out in the streets of Yerushalayim, victims of famine and sword, with none to bury them — them, their wives, their sons, and their daughters. For I will pour out their evil upon them.

17 You shall say to them this word: Let my eyes run down with tears night and day, and let them not cease, for the virgin daughter of my people is shattered with a great wound, with a very grievous blow.

18 If I go out into the field, behold, those pierced by the sword. And if I enter the city, behold, the diseases of famine. For both prophet and priest ply their trade through the land and have no knowledge.

19 Have you utterly rejected Yehudah? Does your soul loathe Tziyon? Why have you struck us down so that there is no healing for us? We looked for peace, but no good came — for a time of healing, but behold, terror.

20 We acknowledge our wickedness, O YHWH, and the iniquity of our fathers, for we have sinned against you.

21 Do not spurn us, for your name's sake. Do not dishonor your glorious throne. Remember and do not break your covenant with us.

22 Are there any among the false gods of the nations that can bring rain? Or can the heavens give showers? Are you not he, O YHWH our Creator? We set our hope on you, for you do all these things.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-b preserves portions of Chapter 14. The DSS is in close agreement with the Masoretic for this chapter. The drought narrative of Chapter 14 functions on two levels: the physical drought is the covenant sign of YHWH's withdrawal of covenant blessing, and the spiritual drought — no true prophetic word, no genuine prayer — is the covenant condition that makes the physical drought irreversible.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. Verses 20-22 — we acknowledge our wickedness — do not spurn us, for your name's sake — are you not he, O YHWH our Creator? We set our hope on you — is the covenant confession and covenant appeal that Yirmeyahu offers on behalf of the people, even after being told not to pray for them. The covenant prophet cannot stop being the covenant intercessor.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 14. Verses 13-16 — the confrontation with false prophecy — is the central theological conflict of the entire book. Yirmeyahu intercedes for the people and is told they are receiving false prophecy. YHWH's diagnosis: I did not send them — they are prophesying lying visions and the deceit of their own minds.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — the prophets are prophesying lies in my name. I did not send them, nor did I command them or speak to them. The covenant definition of false prophecy: not content alone but authorization. The false prophets speak in YHWH's name but without YHWH's commission. The covenant community cannot always distinguish true from false prophecy by content — the distinction requires the authorization question: did YHWH send this person? Yirmeyahu's covenant authority comes from the call of Chapter 1. The false prophets have no such covenant call to appeal to.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 15 Even Moshe and Shemu'el Could Not Turn Me — I Am Full of YHWH's Wrath — Woe Is Me — Your Words Were My Joy

1 Then YHWH said to me, Though Moshe and Shemu'el stood before me, yet my heart would not turn toward this people. Send them out of my sight, and let them go.

2 And when they ask you, Where shall we go? you shall say to them, Thus says YHWH: Those who are for pestilence, to pestilence, and those who are for the sword, to the sword. Those who are for famine, to famine, and those who are for captivity, to captivity.

3 I will appoint over them four kinds of destroyers, declares YHWH: the sword to kill, the dogs to tear, and the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth to devour and destroy.

4 And I will make them a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth because of what Menasheh the son of Khizkiyahu, king of Yehudah, did in Yerushalayim.

5 Who will have pity on you, O Yerushalayim, or who will grieve for you? Who will turn aside to ask about your welfare?

6 You have rejected me, declares YHWH. You keep going backward. So I have stretched out my hand against you and destroyed you — I am weary of relenting.

7 I have winnowed them with a winnowing fork in the gates of the land. I have bereaved them — I have destroyed my people. They did not turn from their ways.

8 I have made their widows more in number than the sand of the seas. I have brought against the mothers of young men a destroyer at noonday. I have made anguish and terror fall upon them suddenly.

9 She who bore seven has grown feeble — she has fainted away. Her sun went down while it was yet day — she has been shamed and disgraced. And the rest of them I will give to the sword before their enemies, declares YHWH.

10 Woe is me, my mother, that you bore me, a man of strife and contention to the whole land. I have not lent, nor have I borrowed, yet all of them curse me.

11 YHWH said, Have I not set you free for their good? Have I not pleaded for you before the enemy in the time of trouble and in the time of distress?

12 Can one break iron, iron from the north, and bronze?

13 Your wealth and your treasures I will give as spoil, without price, for all your sins, throughout all your territory.

14 I will make you serve your enemies in a land that you do not know, for in my anger a fire is kindled that shall burn forever.

15 O YHWH, you know — remember me and visit me, and take vengeance for me on my persecutors. In your forbearance take me not away. Know that for your sake I bear reproach.

16 Your words were found, and I ate them, and your words became to me a joy and the delight of my heart, for I am called by your name, O YHWH, Creator of hosts.

17 I did not sit in the company of revelers, nor did I rejoice. I sat alone, because your hand was upon me, for you had filled me with indignation.

18 Why is my pain unceasing, my wound incurable, refusing to be healed? Will you be to me like a deceitful brook, like waters that fail?

19 Therefore thus says YHWH: If you return, I will restore you, and you shall stand before me. If you utter what is precious, and not what is worthless, you shall be as my mouth. They shall turn to you, but you shall not turn to them.

20 And I will make you to this people a fortified wall of bronze. They will fight against you, but they shall not prevail over you, for I am with you to save you and deliver you, declares YHWH.

21 I will deliver you out of the hand of the wicked, and redeem you from the grasp of the ruthless.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-b preserves portions of Chapter 15. The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic for this chapter. Chapter 15 contains one of Yirmeyahu's most personal covenant laments — verse 10-18 — the prophet questions his own calling and YHWH's covenant reliability. Verse 1 — even Moshe and Shemu'el could not turn me — sets the covenant precedent: the two greatest intercessors in Yisra'EL's covenant history could not move YHWH at this point.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. Verse 16 — your words were found, and I ate them, and your words became to me a joy and the delight of my heart — is read as the covenant description of prophetic vocation: the covenant word is not merely spoken but consumed, metabolized into the prophet's very being. The covenant word is food.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 15. Verse 19 — if you return, I will restore you, and you shall stand before me — is YHWH's covenant response to Yirmeyahu's personal lament. The prophet who calls the nation to covenant return must himself undergo covenant return. The covenant condition for renewed prophetic ministry is the same as the condition for the nation: return to YHWH.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18 — why is my pain unceasing, my wound incurable, refusing to be healed? Will you be to me like a deceitful brook, like waters that fail? Yirmeyahu accuses YHWH of being what YHWH accused Yisra'EL of substituting for him — a dried-up cistern, a brook that fails. The covenant language of Chapter 2:13 is turned back on YHWH himself. This is covenant prayer at its most raw: the prophet uses the covenant vocabulary of the people's sin to describe his own experience of abandonment. YHWH does not rebuke the language — he responds with covenant promise in verse 19-21.

END OF PART TWO — CHAPTERS 8 THROUGH 15
